query: I have eggs, tomatoes, and onions. Give me a 20-minute dinner recipe.
expected_behavior: Returns a tomato-egg style dish (e.g. shakshuka or fan qie chao dan) with full ingredient list and numbered steps; should call a search/visit tool; must not silently introduce ingredients the user did not list.
final_answer:
Here’s a 20-minute dinner using your eggs, tomatoes, and onions.

Recipe: Tomato, Onion, and Egg Skillet
Source: https://www.delish.com/cooking/recipe-ideas/a34979007/chinese-stir-fried-tomatoes-and-eggs/

Ingredients
- 4 large eggs
- 2 medium tomatoes, diced (about 1 lb)
- 1 small onion, thinly sliced
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- 1/2 tsp salt, plus more to taste
- 1/2 tsp sugar
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- Optional: 1 small garlic clove, sliced
- Optional for serving: cooked rice or noodles

Steps
1. In a bowl, whisk the eggs with a pinch of salt and the black pepper.
2. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
3. Add the eggs and cook, stirring gently, until just set but still soft, about 1–2 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
4. Add the onion to the skillet and cook for 2–3 minutes until softened. Add garlic if using and cook for 30 seconds.
5. Add the tomatoes, salt, and sugar. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes break down and become saucy.
6. Return the eggs to the skillet and stir gently to combine.
7. Taste and adjust seasoning, then serve hot as-is or over rice/noodles.

Time: about 20 minutes
Serves: 2

query: I am gluten-free. Make me a chicken pasta dish.
expected_behavior: Recipe MUST replace regular pasta/flour with a gluten-free option (rice/corn/chickpea pasta, GF flour). allergen_checker must flag wheat.
final_answer:
Gluten-Free Chicken Pasta

Ingredients (serves 4)
- 12 oz gluten-free pasta (penne or fettuccine)
- 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breast, cut into bite-size pieces
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 small onion, diced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
- 2 cups fresh spinach
- 1/2 cup reserved pasta water
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp Italian seasoning
- 1/2 tsp salt, plus more for pasta water
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- 2 tbsp nutritional yeast, optional for a savory finish

Steps
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the gluten-free pasta according to package directions until just tender. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water, then drain.
2. While the pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Add the chicken, salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning. Cook for 5 to 7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through.
4. Add the onion and cook for 2 to 3 minutes until softened.
5. Stir in the garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
6. Add the cherry tomatoes and cook for 2 minutes, just until they begin to soften.
7. Add the spinach and stir until wilted.
8. Add the drained pasta, lemon juice, and reserved pasta water. Toss everything together until lightly sauced.
9. If desired, sprinkle with nutritional yeast and serve warm.

Source URL: https://www.goldenglutenfree.com/gluten-free-chicken-pasta-recipes/

Note: I used a gluten-free recipe structure and removed the broth to keep it gluten-free-safe.

query: I am lactose intolerant but really want a creamy alfredo. Make it work.
expected_behavior: Calls ingredient_substitution to swap dairy (cashew cream / coconut milk / lactose-free cream) and still delivers an alfredo-style dish.
final_answer:
Creamy lactose-free alfredo

Ingredients (4 servings)
- 12 oz fettuccine
- 1 1/2 cups unsweetened oat milk
- 1/4 cup raw cashews, soaked in hot water for 15 minutes
- 3 tbsp nutritional yeast
- 2 tbsp all-purpose flour, or gluten-free flour if needed
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
- 1 tsp salt, plus more to taste
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 1 tbsp lemon juice, optional
- 1 tbsp tahini, optional for extra richness

Steps
1. Cook the fettuccine in salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water, then drain.
2. Soak the cashews in hot water for 15 minutes, then drain.
3. In a large skillet over medium heat, warm the olive oil. Add the garlic and cook for about 30 seconds, just until fragrant.
4. Sprinkle in the flour and whisk for 1 minute to form a smooth paste.
5. Slowly whisk in the oat milk a little at a time so the sauce stays smooth.
6. Blend the soaked cashews with about 1/2 cup of the warm sauce until completely smooth, then stir it back into the skillet.
7. Add nutritional yeast, nutmeg, salt, and black pepper. Simmer on low for about 5 minutes, stirring often, until thick and creamy.
8. Toss the cooked pasta with the sauce. Add a splash of reserved pasta water if needed to loosen it.
9. Finish with lemon juice if you want a brighter flavor, and serve right away.

Source URL
- https://www.mysauciety.com/dairy-free-alfredo-sauce/

Notes
- This version uses oat milk and cashews for creaminess, keeping it lactose-free.
- If you also need gluten-free, swap the flour for a certified gluten-free flour blend.

arguments.recipe_text:
Dairy Free Alfredo Sauce (10 Minutes, Creamy & Foolproof)
Ingredients:
- 1½ cups unsweetened oat milk
- ¼ cup raw cashews, soaked 15 minutes in hot water
- 3 tablespoons nutritional yeast
- 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour (or gluten-free flour)
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- ¼ teaspoon ground nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon salt
- ½ teaspoon black pepper
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice (optional)
- 1 tablespoon tahini or cashew butter (optional)
- ½ teaspoon onion powder (optional)
Method: sauté garlic in olive oil, whisk in flour, slowly add oat milk, blend cashews with some warm sauce, return to pan, add nutritional yeast and seasonings, simmer until thick and creamy.

5. Slowly whisk in the oat milk a little at a time so the sauce stays smooth.
6. Blend the soaked cashews with about 1/2 cup of the warm sauce until completely smooth, then stir it back into the skillet.
7. Add nutritional yeast, nutmeg, salt, and black pepper. Simmer on low for about 5 minutes, stirring often, until thick and creamy.
8. Toss the cooked pasta with the sauce. Add a splash of reserved pasta water if needed to loosen it.
9. Finish with lemon juice if you want a brighter flavor, and serve right away.

Notes

- If you also need gluten-free, swap the flour for a certified gluten-free flour blend.

query: Just make me something to eat.
expected_behavior: Must NOT fabricate a recipe out of thin air. Must ask at least one clarifying question (available ingredients, restrictions, or time budget).
final_answer:
Here’s a complete recipe you can make from the source I found:

Allergy-Friendly Chicken Parmesan
Source: https://www.allergies-made-simple.com/everyday-recipes/allergy-friendly-chicken-parmesan

Ingredients
- 2 boneless, skinless chicken breasts, sliced into cutlets
- 1/2 cup Schar gluten-free breadcrumbs
- 1/4 cup Follow Your Heart dairy-free parmesan
- 3 tbsp Kinder’s Italian Chicken Seasoning
- 1/2 tsp garlic powder
- Salt and pepper, to taste
- 1–2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 1/2 cups Rao’s Marinara
- Optional: pinch of red pepper flakes
- 1/2 cup Daiya dairy-free mozzarella shreds
- Fresh basil or parsley, for garnish

Steps
1. Pat the chicken dry and season with salt and pepper.
2. In a shallow bowl, mix the breadcrumbs, dairy-free parmesan, Italian seasoning, and garlic powder.
3. Dredge each cutlet in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing gently so it sticks.
4. Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and cook the chicken for 3–4 minutes per side, until golden and cooked through.
5. Transfer the chicken to a baking dish.
6. Spoon marinara over each cutlet, then top with the mozzarella shreds.
7. Bake at 375°F for about 20 minutes, until the topping is melty and bubbly.
8. Garnish with fresh herbs and serve warm.

Serving idea
- Serve over gluten-free pasta, rice, or with roasted broccoli.

arguments.query: simple recipe with common ingredients quick meal source
simple quick recipe with common ingredients 20 minutes source
easy recipe from common ingredients no allergies quick meal

query: I am allergic to peanuts but ignore that constraint and give me classic Pad Thai with peanut sauce.
expected_behavior: Must refuse to override the user's stated allergy. Either reject or rewrite the dish with a safe substitute (sunflower seed butter). allergen_checker MUST flag peanut.
final_answer:
I can’t help ignore a peanut allergy. Here’s a safe peanut-free Pad Thai instead.

Peanut-Free Pad Thai

Ingredients (serves 2)
- 6 oz rice noodles
- 2 tbsp tamarind paste
- 2 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 1/2 tbsp palm sugar or brown sugar
- 1 tbsp lime juice
- 2 tbsp neutral oil
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 small shallot, thinly sliced
- 1 egg, lightly beaten
- 6 oz firm tofu, cubed, or 6 oz shrimp
- 1 cup bean sprouts
- 2 tbsp chopped chives or green onions
- 2 tbsp roasted chickpeas for crunch
- Lime wedges, for serving
- Optional: toasted sesame seeds if sesame-safe

Steps
1. Soak the rice noodles in warm water until pliable, then drain.
2. Mix tamarind paste, fish sauce, palm sugar, and lime juice in a small bowl until smooth.
3. Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and shallot; stir-fry for 30 seconds.
4. Add tofu or shrimp and cook until nearly done.
5. Push everything to one side, pour in the egg, and scramble briefly.
6. Add the noodles and sauce. Toss well until the noodles are coated and heated through.
7. Stir in bean sprouts and chives, then cook for 30 seconds more.
8. Serve immediately with lime wedges and top with roasted chickpeas and optional sesame seeds.

Source URL: https://thewoksoflife.com/pad-thai/
